rulings.Case Title: SHAWN HOLLE VS. EDWARD OCHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Diallah Bhanji
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 08, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF SHAWN HOLLE's Motion For Protective Order. Plaintiff's motion for protective order is granted in part. Defendant may conduct an additional deposition of plaintiff not to exceed two hours. Plaintiff has already appeared for deposition and this court's summary judgment/adjudication ruling has narrowed the issues in this case. Mr. Ochi shall not appear at the deposition. Plaintiff's request for a discovery referee is denied. The parties and counsel are advised to behave professionally at the deposition. Recalcitrant conduct may lead to sanctions and/or a further deposition. Sanctions denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BRODY M.D. VS. DONE GLOBAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, December 08, 2025, Line 12. PLAINTIFF DAVID BRODY's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Plainitff's motion to strike Defendant Done Global, Inc.'s Answer to Amended Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff's motion fails to demonstrate that Plaintiff met and conferred with Defendant prior to filing the motion as required by Code of Civil Procedure 435.5. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
